title: anachortus divaricatus subsp. articulatus
intro: Anachortus divaricatus subsp. articulatus, also known as the mountain winter aconite, is a subspecies of the Anachortus divaricatus plant, a type of flowering plant in the Aconitum genus. The plant contains aconitine, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. Anachortus divaricatus subsp. articulatus has been used to relieve pain and inflammation, and its health benefits include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its roots and leaves, often dried or processed into tinctures, infusions, or ointments, although caution and expert guidance are advised due to its toxicity.
